verify:prior-art.additions: "(1) coi-serviceworker escape hatch: gzuidhof/coi-serviceworker (MIT-licensed, npm-installable) enables cross-origin isolation on GitHub Pages via a service worker, making SharedArrayBuffer multi-threaded Stockfish feasible if StockThink ever wants it; caveats: one-time reload on first load, file must be served same-origin and unbundled, HTTPS required. (2) lichess cloud-eval operational realities verified live: keyless and CORS-usable, but it is a CACHE — uncached FENs return 404, so a local-engine fallback is mandatory (freechess treats cloud as opportunistic and marks position.worker='cloud'/'local'); freechess also abandons cloud usage for the rest of the game once a position misses. It returns knodes/depth/pvs, often at very high depth (75 for startpos). (3) Chesskit ships chunked WASM (stockfish-17.1-single-part-0..5.wasm, stockfish-18-single-6563532-part-0..5.wasm) — splitting big engine binaries into ~part files is the proven pattern for staying under GitHub's 100MB per-file limit while hosting full NNUE builds on Pages; the -lite-single 16.1 build is the small-download option. (4) wintrchess shared/src/lib/reporter/utils contains a GPL-3.0 TypeScript+chess.js implementation of exactly the detector layer StockThink needs (pieceSafety.ts, attackers.ts, defenders.ts, dangerLevels.ts, pieceTrapped.ts, criticalMove.ts, opening.ts) plus classification/brilliant.ts — useful as an algorithm reference, but porting verbatim drags in GPL-3.0; OpenChess-Insights (MIT) remains the only license-safe code source. (5) GitHub auto-detects freechess's license as NOASSERTION ('Other') because CC licenses aren't recognized — anyone auditing StockThink's provenance must read the LICENSE text, not the API field. (6) OpenChess-Insights' accuracy uses NON-lichess constants: 100.0307234*exp(-0.1008298*winDelta) - 0.03076726 per move (the lichess 103.1668/-0.04354 formula appears only in a comment), win% via ±0.00368208, accuracy 100 when winDelta <= 0; its mate commentary keys off string sentinels from calculate_points_gained_by_move ('mates n', 'gets mated n', 'continues gets mated n', 'lost mate') with evals pinned at ±10000 — a clean state machine to replicate for mate commentary. (7) freechess client spawns a dedicated 'cutoffWorker' that first evaluates the FINAL position at full depth before the batch loop — a practical trick so the eval bar endpoint is correct early; its depth slider buckets UX at <=14 (fast/bolt icon) and <=17 (medium/wind icon). (8) freechess issue #13 confirms brilliant false positives are an acknowledged unresolved problem in the simplest sacrifice-detection approach — Chesskit's getIsPieceSacrifice + alternative-line guard and wintrchess's pieceSafety approach are the two evolved designs to study. (9) The retro/learn-from-mistakes flow requires prev.hasCompChild (a computer solution branch must exist in the tree) — meaning StockThink must store the engine PV as a child variation per blunder node to replicate the feature."

research:Programmatic chess-concept dete.summary: Concept detection for StockThink should be built by porting the lichess-puzzler tagger (ornicar/lichess-puzzler/tagger/cook.py + util.py, MIT-licensed Python on python-chess) to chessops in TypeScript. This is production code that tags millions of lichess puzzles with the exact motifs StockThink needs: fork, pin (two variants), skewer, discovered attack/check, sacrifice, hanging piece, trapped piece, back-rank mate, double check, x-ray, advanced pawn/promotion. All detectors are pure board-state functions built on five primitives — attacks(square), attackers(color, square), SquareSet.between(a,b), pin direction, and piece values {P:1,N:3,B:3,R:5,Q:9,K:99} — every one of which exists in chessops (attacks(), attacksTo(), between(), ray(), and the sniper/blocker pin computation in Position.ctx()). The foundational helpers are is_defended (with x-ray defender handling: temporarily remove a ray attacker and re-check), is_hanging = !is_defended, is_in_bad_spot (attacked AND (hanging OR attackable by lower-value piece)), and is_trapped (no escape move leads to a safe square). Sacrifice detection in cook.py is simply material_diff dropping ≥2 points below the starting diff along the line without promotion compensating; freechess (WintrCat/freechess) instead detects Brilliant by scanning the mover's non-king/non-pawn pieces for any that are newly hanging (isPieceHanging with attacker/defender counting) and worth more than what was captured. For exchange soundness, the chessprogramming.org iterative SEE swap algorithm (gain-array negamax with x-ray reintroduction) is the standard; implement it once over chessops bitboards and use it for "loses material to capture", sacrifice confirmation, and hanging-piece verification. The engine PV is the primary explanation source: python-chess-annotator's pattern is (a) annotate only when winning-chances delta > 7.5% using winning% = 50 + 50*(2/(1+e^(-0.004*cp))-1), (b) attach the refutation PV truncated to 10 plies (extended to game end if it ends the game), (c) end-of-variation comment is "Mate in X", eval/depth, or a game-end label; NAG thresholds ??≤-300cp, ?≤-150cp, ?!≤-75cp. For commentary structure, academia converges on the same taxonomy: Jhamtani et al. 2018 (GAMEKNOT, ~298K move-comment pairs, code at harsh19/ChessCommentaryGeneration) and Zang et al. 2019 categorize comments as Description / Quality / Comparison / Planning / Context; DecodeChess structures explanations as Threats, Good Moves, Plans, Piece Functionality, and Concepts. McGrath et al. (AlphaZero probing) used Stockfish 8 eval heads (material, imbalance, mobility, king_safety, threats, passed_pawns, space) plus 116 binary custom concepts (has_mate_threat, in_check, can_capture_queen_opponent, has_contested_open_file) — these binary concepts are the template for StockThink's per-position "fact" extractors. Book detection: lichess-org/chess-openings (CC0) ships a–e.tsv with eco/name/pgn columns; the dist/ build adds uci and epd columns — match positions by EPD against a Map for transposition-safe detection, and a move is "Book" while the reached position is in the table; opening name = last matched entry. Game-phase detection for endgame commentary: lichess scalachess Divider — middlegame when majors+minors ≤ 10 or back rank sparse (<4 pieces) or mixedness > 150; endgame when majors+minors ≤ 6. Simple endgame facts (pawn endgame, opposite-colored bishops, material edge) fall out of piece counts.

key_facts.claim: lichess-puzzler tagger (tagger/cook.py) contains working Python detectors for fork, pin_prevents_attack, pin_prevents_escape, skewer, discovered_attack, discovered_check, sacrifice, hanging_piece, trapped_piece, back_rank_mate, double_check, x_ray, advanced_pawn, promotion, under_promotion — directly portable to chessops/TS

Fork detection (lichess): after a non-king move to a square that is not 'in a bad spot', count attacked enemy non-pawn pieces that are either higher-valued than the moved piece OR (hanging AND not themselves attacking the forking square); fork if count > 1

is_defended handles x-ray defense: if a square's attacker is a ray piece (B/R/Q), remove it from a board copy and re-check whether the piece's own side then attacks the square; is_hanging = not is_defended
is_trapped (lichess): piece is not pawn/king, not pinned, in a bad spot, and every legal escape move either captures a lower-value piece or lands on another bad spot
Sacrifice (lichess): material_diff (P1 N3 B3 R5 Q9) on player's move nodes drops <= initial-2 at any point in the line, and no later player promotion exists

Skewer (lichess): a capture by a ray piece (B/R/Q) where the opponent's previous move originated from a square between from/to of the capture, and the opponent's moved piece was higher-valued than the piece captured behind it
Discovered check (lichess): position is check and the checker set does NOT contain the moved-to square (the checking piece is not the piece that moved)

python-chess board.pin() equivalent in chessops: Position.ctx() computes blockers via snipers = (rookAttacks(king, empty) ∩ rooksAndQueens) ∪ (bishopAttacks(king, empty) ∩ bishopsAndQueens) of opponent; a piece is pinned if it is the single occupied square between king and sniper

chessops provides attacks(piece, square, occupied), between(a,b), ray(a,b), kingAttacks/knightAttacks/pawnAttacks/bishopAttacks/rookAttacks/queenAttacks, and attacksTo(square, color, board, occupied) — sufficient primitives to port all cook.py detectors

python-chess-annotator: annotate a move only when winning-chances delta > 7.5 percentage points, where winning% = 50 + 50*(2/(1+exp(-0.004*cp))-1); NAG thresholds in cp delta: blunder < -300, mistake < -150, dubious < -75

python-chess-annotator comment pattern: add refutation PV as a variation truncated to 10 half-moves (unless line ends the game), terminal comment is 'Mate in X' or 'eval/depth' or game-end label (Stalemate/Fifty move rule/Three-fold repetition/Insufficient material)

freechess Brilliant detection: after the move, scan mover's non-king non-pawn pieces; if any isPieceHanging(lastFen, fen, square) and its value exceeds the value captured by the move, classify BRILLIANT; Great move requires opponent's previous move was BLUNDER, gap between top two engine moves >= 150cp, and moved-to piece not hanging

freechess isPieceHanging: hanging if any attacker is lower-valued than the piece, or attackers > defenders with exceptions (defended by pawn; piece cheaper than min attacker with a cheap defender; rook 'attacked' only by one minor when a minor was just traded)

Iterative SEE swap algorithm: gain[d] = value[attacker] - gain[d-1] speculative store, remove used attacker from occupancy, re-add x-ray attackers (pawns|bishops|rooks|queens behind), pick least valuable attacker each ply, then back-propagate gain[d-1] = -max(-gain[d-1], gain[d])

lichess-org/chess-openings: five TSV files a.tsv–e.tsv with columns eco/name/pgn; dist/ build (make, or CI artifacts) adds uci and epd columns; CC0 public domain; book detection should match by EPD position (transposition-safe), 'play moves backwards until a named position is found' for naming

lichess game-phase Divider: middlegame entry when majorsAndMinors <= 10 OR backrank sparse (<4 pieces on a side's first rank) OR mixedness > 150; endgame entry when majorsAndMinors <= 6 (pieces excluding kings and pawns)

McGrath et al. (AlphaZero) probed Stockfish 8 eval concepts material, imbalance, pawns, knights, bishops, rooks, queens, mobility, king_safety, threats, passed_pawns, space, total, plus 116 custom binary concepts incl. has_mate_threat, in_check, can_capture_queen_opponent, has_contested_open_file — a ready-made checklist of per-position boolean facts for commentary

Jhamtani et al. 2018 GAMEKNOT dataset: ~298K move-commentary pairs from ~11K games; code/crawler at github.com/harsh19/ChessCommentaryGeneration (no license file; data must be re-crawled); commentary classified into categories incl. move description, move quality, comparative, planning, contextual

Zang et al. 2019 (ACL) generate commentary in five categories — Description, Quality, Comparison, Planning, Contexts — grounded in an internal engine's move predictions and evaluations; supports the same five-slot template structure for rule-based output

DecodeChess (proprietary, Stockfish-based XAI) structures each explanation as: Threats, Good Moves (ranked), Plans, piece Functionality, and Concepts — a proven UX blueprint for the 'explain more' panel

No off-the-shelf JS/TS tactics-detection library exists; chess.js ecosystem only does move-gen/validation, so porting cook.py is the right approach. JS SEE references exist in engines (kbjorklu/chess, Garbochess-JS)

research:Programmatic chess-concept dete.recommendation: Build StockThink's concept detector as a TypeScript port of lichess-puzzler's cook.py/util.py onto chessops (not chess.js — chessops has the bitboard primitives: attacks(), attacksTo(), between(), ray(), and sniper/blocker pin logic; chess.js lacks attackers/pin APIs and freechess's FEN-mangling getAttackers workaround is slow and hacky). Layer the system as: (1) primitives — values, materialDiff, isDefended (with x-ray), isHanging, isInBadSpot, isTrapped, plus one iterative SEE (swap algorithm) over chessops SquareSets; (2) motif detectors run not on the played game alone but on short lines: the move just played, the engine PV after the played move (refutation facts: "this hangs the rook to Bxf7+"), and the PV of the best move that was NOT played (missed-tactic facts for Miss/Great commentary) — exactly how cook.py walks puzzle.mainline[1::2]; (3) PV-grounded commentary following python-chess-annotator: only elaborate when winning-chances delta > ~7.5% (winning% = 50+50*(2/(1+e^(-0.004cp))-1)), show the first ~10 plies of the refutation PV, announce "Mate in X" from engine mate scores; (4) template slots per the literature consensus (Description / Quality / Comparison-to-best / Planning / Context) and a DecodeChess-style "explain more" panel listing Threats (hanging/forkable pieces found by detectors), the engine's top alternatives, and active Concepts (pins, passed pawns, back-rank weakness, trapped pieces); (5) Book detection via lichess-org/chess-openings dist build (CC0): precompile the TSVs into a JSON Map keyed by EPD at build time (~3500 entries, a few hundred KB), label moves Book while position is in the map, name the opening from the deepest hit; (6) game phases via the scalachess Divider thresholds (middlegame: majors+minors<=10 || backrank sparse || mixedness>150; endgame: majors+minors<=6) to gate phase-appropriate commentary, with simple piece-count facts (pure pawn endgame, opposite bishops, up-the-exchange) for endgame remarks. Use lichess piece values (P1 N3 B3 R5 Q9 K99) everywhere for consistency with the ported detectors. For sacrifice/Brilliant, prefer cook.py's material_diff-along-PV definition (robust) cross-checked with SEE, and keep freechess's isPieceHanging exceptions (pawn-defended, minor-for-rook) as guards against false positives. License note: lichess-puzzler and scalachess are open source (verify headers: puzzler is AGPL-3.0 — port the algorithms/ideas and cite, or reimplement from the published logic rather than copying verbatim into a permissively-licensed repo); chess-openings data is CC0 and safe to bundle.

corrections: MID-SEARCH BEHAVIOR claim is wrong in two ways. (1) Lila does NOT emit/re-render 'on every parsed streaming info line': protocol.ts only updates currentEval when `depth === (this.currentEval?.depth ?? 0) + 1` and only emits when `multiPv === this.expectedPvs && this.currentEval` — i.e. once per completed depth/multipv set. (2) There IS JS-side rate limiting: ui/lib/src/ceval/ctrl.ts line 85: `onEmit: (ev: LocalEval, work: Work) => void = throttle(200, (ev, work) => { ... this.opts.emit(ev, work); ... })` — a 200ms throttle between UI updates. The 'no JS interpolation/easing' part is true (smoothing is the 1s CSS transition only), but the emission cadence description must be corrected. Sources: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lichess-org/lila/master/ui/lib/src/ceval/protocol.ts and .../ctrl.ts

ISSUE #11026 RATIONALE is wrong. The issue exists ('Use the engine's WDL values for the eval bar', opened by dav1312 2022-06-17, now CLOSED), but the 'material/ply dependence means same cp fills bar differently and jumps on captures' rationale does NOT appear in the thread. The actual maintainer rationale (ornicar, 2024-01-31): 'Lichess eval bars already use a winning chances function. It targets the chances for a human to win the game, not for an engine. I think it's more useful.' Notably dav1312 argued FOR WDL, pointing out lichess's constant 'hasn't been updated in almost 2 years even tho Stockfish's evaluation was redefined' and is fit only on 2300+ humans. Source: https://api.github.com/repos/lichess-org/lila/issues/11026/comments

SF 15.1 NORMALIZATION claim conflates two eras. Normalization did start with SF 15.1 ('+1 evaluation = 50% chance of winning against an equally strong opponent', per the 15.1 release blog), but SF 15.1 used a CONSTANT divisor (UCI::to_cp(v) = 100*v/NormalizeToPawnValue, a single int constant), NOT the quoted material-dependent `UCIEngine::to_cp(v, pos) = round(100 * v / a)` — that material-dependent form (win_rate_params polynomial in clamped material) is the current-master implementation introduced later (SF 17 era, 2024). The quoted formula is correct for current master only. Sources: https://stockfishchess.org/blog/2022/stockfish-15-1/ and https://github.com/official-stockfish/Stockfish/blob/master/src/uci.cpp

CHESS.COM SUPPORT ARTICLE claim over-attributes. The article at support.chess.com/en/articles/8379354 explains only positive/negative pawn-unit meaning; it does NOT mention forced-mate display (+M#/-M#) or the bar being fully one color — that part of the claim is unsupported by the cited source (the rendering detail is observable in their product but not documented there). Core fact stands: no published formula. Source: https://support.chess.com/en/articles/8379354-computer-evaluation-numbers-on-chess-com-like-2-25

FREECHESS totalHeight sharpening: totalHeight is not a hardcoded constant in evalbar.ts — it is `const totalHeight = evaluationBar.clientHeight;` read ONCE at script load from the SVG element, which has height="730" in src/public/pages/report/index.html; separately, '730' is hardcoded as string literals inside setEvalBarToWinner. The ±1095cp saturation figure is correct given the 730px height. (Gotcha for implementers: clientHeight-at-load breaks if the bar is hidden/resized at load.) Source: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/WintrCat/freechess/master/src/public/pages/report/scripts/evalbar.ts and .../report/index.html

UCI SPEC quote nuance: the spec literally says 'If the engine is getting mated use negativ values for y' (typo 'negativ' in the original); also the spec only says 'from the engine's point of view' — 'side to move at the search-root position' is the universal de-facto interpretation (and what Stockfish does, as lila's ply-parity flip confirms), but it is not verbatim spec text. Source: https://www.wbec-ridderkerk.nl/html/UCIProtocol.html lines ~299-302
verify:eval-bar.additions: CRITICAL MISSED FACTS: (1) Lichess Accuracy formula on the same accuracy page (needed for StockThink's accuracy score): Accuracy% = 103.1668 * exp(-0.04354 * (winPercentBefore - winPercentAfter)) - 3.1669 — verbatim from https://lichess.org/page/accuracy. (2) winningChances.ts also exports povDiff = (povChances(e1) - povChances(e2)) / 2 (move-quality delta in [-1,1], the input to lichess inaccuracy/mistake/blunder thresholds) plus two ready-made similarity thresholds: areSimilarEvals uses povDiff < 0.14, and hasMultipleSolutions uses povChances >= 0.3524 ('cp equivalent >= 200') — useful calibration anchors for move classification. (3) lila ctrl.ts sorts multipv lines by povChances (sortPvsInPlace, line 284-285), not by raw cp — mate lines sort correctly above cp lines because of the mate-to-cp-equivalent mapping. (4) getBestEval (view/main.ts) prefers local engine eval over server eval: `ctrl.getNode().ceval ?? (showFishnetAnalysis ? ctrl.getNode().eval : undefined)` — pattern for mixing cached/PGN evals with live engine output. (5) nmrugg/stockfish.js is GPL-3.0; a GitHub Pages app bundling it must ship the license and source offer (compatible with a free static app, but the whole app effectively must be GPL-compatible). (6) Stockfish's WDL/normalization model is maintained at github.com/official-stockfish/WDL_model (referenced in the uci.cpp comment) — coefficients ARE refit between releases, so hardcoding as[]/bs[] client-side will drift from whatever WASM build is shipped; better to parse the engine's own 'wdl' info field with UCI_ShowWDL=true (confirmed present in stockfish.js) if WDL is wanted. (7) Consistency warning for StockThink: lichess's -0.00368208 was fit in mid-2022 against engine cp of that era; SF >= 15.1 cp is win-prob-anchored and rescaled since (dav1312's point in #11026: lichess never refit). Lichess knowingly accepts this drift — a clone can too, but should not mix the lichess sigmoid with engine WDL per-mille in the same pipeline. (8) renderGauge hides the gauge entirely for ongoing games (ctrl.ongoing guard) and lichess's eval text display elsewhere caps/format differs from the gauge — the gauge consumes only povChances. (9) Emission cadence to copy: update eval only when depth increments by exactly 1 and the multipv set is complete, throttle UI updates at 200ms, and let a CSS `transition: height 1s` do all visual smoothing — that is the complete lichess recipe, no JS easing anywhere.
